work_item.description: Harden the PreToolUse lease guardrail against Bash bypass. The hook's matcher is "Write|Edit", so it only inspects the Write/Edit tools; any file mutation performed via Bash (sed, python, tee, shell redirection) bypasses the lease check entirely, defeating "all product source edits must proceed through TDD" for product repos. A PreToolUse prompt hook cannot reliably parse arbitrary shell to know which file a command writes. Proposed approaches: (a) a PostToolUse validator that diffs the working tree and rejects/flags un-leased changes to product src/ after the fact; and/or (b) enforce the lease server-side in the cfk kernel (e.g. on cf_submit / event append) so enforcement does not depend on which client tool performed the write. Acceptance: an un-leased edit to product src/ via Bash is detected and surfaced. Related: the new .claude-factory/LEASE_BYPASS sentinel-file mechanism (per-project temporary opt-out) added to hooks.json.
